title: Gymnogramma ceterach
intro: Gymnogramma ceterach, also known as the common prick-madam or ceterach, is a type of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in reducing fever, treating wounds, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma ceterach include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gymnogramma ceterach is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Gymnogramma ceterach is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is part of the genus Gymnogramma, which comprises several species of ferns. The classification of Gymnogramma ceterach further divides it into the kingdom Plantae, the division Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found worldwide. Gymnogramma ceterach is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas.
